The Regulations of Football are an official set of rules governing football for England. They are the most popular version and are observed across all major English clubs throughout the game of football in England. Although there are many variations, the rules state that for every goal scored by a team in a match of regulation football, twice a winner is awarded based on the aggregate number of goals scored. Two national governing bodies, the Football Association and the Football Association Executive Board have separate versions of the Regulations of Football covering variations in almost every aspect of the game.
